title: Stage 1: LinkedIn Connections
subtitle: This graph comes from the fresh six-connection MiroFish run and starts with several credible warm routes already present in the founder's LinkedIn connections.
callouts: Harrison and Justin form the direct investor layer. Katia, Sally, Matt, and Anastasiia form the softer operator and platform bridge layer.

likelyInvestorDestination.name: Hack VC, Decasonic, and platform-led paths
likelyInvestorDestination.description: The system is still mapping which route is warmest and safest.

narrative.warmPathSummary: Tigerclaw begins by proving there is already a real investor surface across two layers: the founder's first-degree network and the second-degree surface revealed through the top five strongest connections.
narrative.whyThisPerson: Harrison and Justin anchor the direct investor layer, while Katia, Sally, Matt, and Anastasiia represent softer operator and ecosystem bridges whose own first-degree networks create the second-degree expansion layer.
narrative.whyNow: This snapshot matters before any reasoning because it proves the founder does not need net-new prospecting to start. The first-degree network already contains the entry points, and the top five connections unlock the adjacent second-degree surface.
narrative.whyThisRankedAboveOthers: At this point nothing is final. The shortlist is being preserved so MiroFish can pressure-test both direct venture routes and connector-first bridges without prematurely hiding viable options.
narrative.suggestedAsk: Do not send yet. Move into the reasoning stages and tighten the path before drafting any outreach.
supportingProof: Harrison at Hack VC and Justin at Decasonic already form real investor-facing first-degree routes.
Katia, Sally, Matt, and Anastasiia create softer operator and platform bridges whose own networks extend the second-degree surface.
The graph already contains enough first-degree and second-degree warm structure to start with strategy instead of cold prospecting.
narrative.likelyObjection: If the founder chooses too early, the product will look like keyword ranking instead of strategic path selection.
narrative.nextBestStep: Narrow the graph into one primary investor route and one softer bridge route.
narrative.draftMessage: No single outreach draft is active in shortlist mode. Advance to Stage 2 or Stage 3 to see the ask tighten around one specific path.

executionRows.label: Stage mode
executionRows.value: Shortlist view only. This stage is about route coverage, not execution.

executionRows.label: Draft posture
executionRows.value: No message should be sent until one route is narrowed and explained.
